description: Live, on-device text recognition — Apple Vision on iOS, ML Kit on Android. The text-scanning sibling to mobile_scanner.
rules: text_sight does live and one-shot on-device text recognition via each platform native engine — Apple Vision on iOS (a system framework, so iOS links zero third-party ML libraries: no GoogleMLKit) and ML Kit on Android. No recognition lib in your pubspec.
Import only the barrel 'package:text_sight/text_sight.dart'; never import from lib/src/ directly.

Live recognition: create a TextSightController, render a TextSightView, then call controller.start() once camera permission is granted (the view does NOT auto-start). Read results via onResult or controller.captures; boxes are normalized [0,1], top-left.
One-shot recognition (no camera, session, or permission): await TextSight.recognizeImage(bytes) or TextSight.recognizePath(path); it defaults to RecognitionLevel.accurate.

Reconfigure a running session through the controller's update* methods — updateRegionOfInterest, updateRecognitionLevel, updateLanguages, updateTorchEnabled — each an async command returning Future<void>, not a set* method or setter.

Platforms: iOS 13.0+ (Vision — modern RecognizeTextRequest on 18+, legacy VNRecognizeTextRequest on 13–17; iOS 13–16 don't follow live device rotation), Android API 24+ (ML Kit Latin). recognitionLevel/languages apply on iOS only; Android Latin ignores them. Region-of-interest is a normalized Rect: iOS restricts Vision to it; Android crops one-shot, live keeps centred lines.

RecognizedLine.confidence is double? in [0,1]; null means the engine supplied none (never synthesized), and the scales are not comparable across platforms — nor across iOS versions (modern RecognizeTextRequest is coarse, often 1.0; legacy VNRecognizeTextRequest is graded).
